title: Inventory: dimension-aware on-hand query through InventDim/InventDimParm
instruction: In the sandbox model, read on-hand inventory the supported way. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="inventory-management"). Create a class DemoOnHandReader with public static methods: (1) `InventQty availableForSite(ItemId _itemId, InventSiteId _siteId)` - builds an InventDim buffer carrying only InventSiteId, finds/creates its canonical record with InventDim::findOrCreate(), sets the matching InventDimParm coverage flags with initFromInventDim(), and reads the quantity through the InventOnHand/InventSumDatePhysicalDim path taught by the knowledge entry; (2) `boolean hasPhysicalOnHand(ItemId _itemId)` - the item-level probe using the same API with an empty dimension set. Selecting InventSum or InventTrans directly and summing PostedQty FAILS this case: dimension coverage must come from InventDimParm, never from a hand-written where clause. Confirm every inventory class and member signature with search/get_method before writing it - do not guess. The class must build with zero BP errors.
